failure_summary: PROPOSAL_08_CYBERSECURITY_20260409T040929Z.md was generated, scored 1.0 by the benchmark runner, ratified by the sovereign, and triggered auto_deploy — yet it contains zero lines of code. Only the YAML frontmatter exists. The benchmark gate accepted an empty proposal as a passing implementation.

failure_modes.root_cause: Benchmark gate has no minimum-substance threshold. Any proposal that parses (even an empty markdown shell with valid frontmatter) is treated as a candidate. Tests on empty modules pass trivially because there's nothing to fail. The model likely ran out of context window or hit a generation timeout, producing only the frontmatter header before stopping.

for_forge_system: Add MIN_CODE_LINES gate: reject proposals with fewer than 20 lines of code in the largest valid block.
Add EXPECTED_NAMES gate: extract expected dataclass/function names from brief INTERFACE CONTRACT, verify proposal AST contains each one before scoring.
Add GENERATION_COMPLETENESS gate: detect if generation stopped mid-token (truncated final line, missing closing delimiters, etc).
for_e14_self_healing_engine: Seed pattern EMPTY_PROPOSAL_PASS with detection signature: proposal file size < 500 bytes AND benchmark score == 1.0.
Resolution action: REGENERATE with explicit minimum-line instruction in brief.

remediation_taken: Phase G deployed pip_audit_gate.py from the earlier PROPOSAL_08_CYBERSECURITY_20260408T120827Z.md proposal which contains real implementation code. The empty 20260409T040929Z proposal is preserved in the vault as forensic evidence.
